job_description:
Sr. B2B Content Specialist

2Base Technologies is at a deliberate stage of building its marketing function, and this role owns the written voice of that function end to end. We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

This is a senior writing role with a specific, demanding purpose. It is not a content management role or a social media role. If that distinction is unclear from the outset, this is probably not the right fit.

What This Role Exists to Do

This role does not invent the voice. It operationalises, applies, and enforces it across all written output: long-form content that earns search traffic, content written in the voice of senior leadership for professional publishing, and the quality standard for everything written that leaves this company.

The audience spans technical leaders and business decision-makers at mid-market companies. CTOs and IT Directors who evaluate architecture decisions, and Founders, COOs, and Operations leaders who are accountable for technology outcomes without always having a technical background. Writing that works across that range, without oversimplifying for one end or losing the other, is the actual craft challenge of this role. Technically credible, commercially relevant, and written with a clear point of view. That is the standard this role is hired to meet and maintain.

What You Will Own

  • All long-form written content: pillar pages, blogs, playbooks, decision frameworks, and use-case libraries targeting senior decision-makers in the US and UK
  • Written content produced in the voice of senior leadership for professional platforms, which requires the ability to write convincingly as someone other than yourself
  • The 2Base content voice, including auditing all published content to ensure it meets the company's brand standards
  • Content briefs written clearly enough that a junior team member can produce a first draft without needing to ask clarifying questions
  • The content quality gate: nothing is published without passing through this role first
  • AI tool usage as a productivity layer, not as a substitute for thinking

What You Will Not Own

  • SEO keyword selection: the SEO Specialist sets this and you write within it
  • Social media scheduling and publishing: a separate team member owns this
  • Visual design of content assets: the Visual Designer owns this
  • Distribution strategy: the Marketing & Growth Lead decides which channels content goes to
  • ICP definition: you write for the ICP and you do not define it

Requirements

Experience - Minimum 3 to 5 years writing B2B content, with strong candidates typically at the 5 to 6 year mark - Has written content that ranked on Google, not just content that was published - Has written in voices other than their own, for executives, founders, or senior professionals - Understands SEO at a working level: knows what search intent means and can write to it without being an SEO specialist - Has worked with AI writing tools and knows both their value and their limits - Can show content that contributed to a measurable outcome: traffic, leads, or engagement with the right audience

Background - Technology, SaaS, or professional services background strongly preferred

Writing Ability

  • Writes with a clear point of view, not neutral summaries of other people's ideas
  • Can explain complex technical concepts in plain language without losing technical credibility
  • Writes concisely with no filler, no padding, and no unnecessary qualification
  • Can write in different voices and make each one sound authentic, not just adjusted

Mindset

  • Treats content as a commercial tool, not a creative exercise
  • Comfortable being edited and does not treat feedback as personal criticism
  • Genuinely curious about AI, technology, and business operations, not faking interest to get the job
  • Fast: this team moves at pace and cannot wait three days for a first draft

What We Are Not Looking For

  • Portfolios of "Top 10 Tips" articles or trend summaries with no original thinking
  • Writers who cannot explain how any of their content performed or what it contributed to
  • Anyone who has never written for a technical or senior business audience and considers that detail irrelevant
  • Writers who only produce self-directed content and have never worked consistently to a brief
  • Anyone who cannot write convincingly in a voice other than their own
  • Candidates who treat AI tools as the primary author and apply only light editing

Benefits

A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Senior B2B Content Specialist required to craft technically credible, commercially relevant content for technical and business leaders. Must enforce brand voice and ensure content meets quality standards.

job_description:
Organic Growth & SEO Specialist

2Base Technologies is building its marketing function from a clear brief, and this role owns the organic search engine that sits at the centre of it. We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

What This Role Exists to Do

This role does not optimise content after it is written. It determines what gets written, why, and how it is structured so that the right people find it at the right moment in their decision process. The organic search function at 2Base is being built to generate qualified inbound pipeline from two specific markets, the US and UK, at scale. That means a large volume of high-intent pages, a technical foundation that performs, and a programmatic architecture that compounds over time. The person who fills this role designs that system and owns its results.

What You Will Own

  • The organic traffic strategy for the US and UK markets, including keyword targeting and the full architecture of how search drives inbound pipeline
  • Keyword research and search intent mapping across all service lines and target audiences
  • Page architecture of the website, including what pages exist, what they target, how they are structured, and how they connect through internal linking
  • On-page SEO across all published pages
  • Technical SEO oversight, working directly with the development team on crawlability, indexing, site speed, Core Web Vitals, and structured data
  • Google Search Console as a working instrument, including weekly review, issue resolution, and opportunity identification
  • The programmatic SEO build, including designing the framework and templates that allow the site to scale to 80 to 120+ high-intent pages
  • SEO briefs written clearly enough that the Content Specialist can execute without guessing
  • Link building target approval. The outreach team identifies candidates, and you approve or reject based on defined quality standards
  • SEO performance reporting, including organic traffic, keyword movement, pages published, and Search Console health

What You Will Not Own

  • Writing content: you brief the Content Specialist, you do not write the pages
  • Link building outreach: a dedicated team member executes this, you set the standard
  • Paid search: that is a separate role
  • Social media distribution: separate channel, separate owner
  • Content calendar scheduling: the Marketing & Growth Lead sets distribution priorities

Requirements

Experience - Minimum 3 to 5 years in an SEO or organic growth role, with strong candidates typically at the 4 to 6 year mark - Experience ranking high-intent commercial pages, not just informational content - Experience managing technical SEO independently - Experience building or contributing to a programmatic SEO architecture - Strong working knowledge of Google Search Console as an active tool - Experience working across US and/or UK markets - Working knowledge of AEO, GEO, and AIO, including structuring content for AI-powered search surfaces - Ability to write SEO briefs that a content writer can execute without clarification

Mindset - Thinks in systems, not tasks. The goal is a compounding organic engine, not a checklist - Comfortable with ambiguity in early-stage environments - Analytically honest, able to diagnose what is not working and adjust direction independently - Collaborative but not passive. Works closely with content and development teams while driving the agenda

What We Are Not Looking For

  • SEO practitioners whose entire experience is informational content with no connection to commercial intent or pipeline
  • Anyone who treats Search Console as a monthly reporting task
  • Candidates who cannot explain the difference between high-intent and informational keyword targeting
  • Candidates whose SEO process depends entirely on tools
  • Candidates with no exposure to US or UK search behaviour

Benefits

  • A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

Contact: If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Strategic SEO leader to build scalable organic growth systems for US/UK markets, focusing on technical execution and high-intent content architecture.

Requirements

  • Experience: 4-8 years
  • Education: Diploma in IT/CS, BSc IT/CS, BCA, B-Tech IT/CS, or similar
  • Skills:
  • Manual testing (web/mobile applications)

job_description:

The Business Analyst plays a key role in ensuring requirements are clearly understood, accurately documented, and traceable throughout the software development lifecycle. Required Skills and Experience - 1 to 3 years of experience in Business Analysis or related roles - Strong experience in BRD and SRS creation - Hands-on experience with documentation templates, standards, and version control - Ability to document UI wireframes and functional flows clearly - Working knowledge of APIs and system integrations for documentation purposes - Experience with project management systems and documentation tools - Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple documents in parallel - Excellent written communication and stakeholder coordination skills

Minimum Qualifications - Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field - 1 to 3 years of experience as a Business Analyst or Product Analyst

Benefits - A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.

At 2Base, we are rebuilding the Business Analyst function into a world-class, product-minded, clarity-driven team. This role is not a documentation role. It is not a screen updater role. It is not a support role. This is a core Business Analyst position responsible for functional modelling, system reasoning, requirement clarity, and building the foundation for correct development.

  • Lead requirement workshops, client calls, and discovery discussions to understand business needs clearly.
  • Ask deeper questions, challenge assumptions, and validate the true business intent behind every requirement.

  • Break large features into modules, workflows, components, and clear dependencies.

  • Push back politely on unclear or changing requirements and ensure final decisions are documented properly.
  • Maintain requirement traceability throughout the lifecycle and ensure zero ambiguity during handover to development.
  • Own the entire requirement lifecycle from discovery to development readiness with high accountability.
  • Drive clarity across teams by ensuring every requirement is complete, validated, and logically consistent.

Required Skills & Experience
